nodes.title: Fail fast on length mismatch
nodes.why: Anagrams must have exactly the same number of characters.
nodes.alternative: Skip this check and rely on frequency map only.
nodes.tradeoff: One quick check saves unnecessary work for obvious non-matches.

nodes.title: Use a clean object for character counts
nodes.why: A plain dictionary with no prototype keys avoids accidental collisions.
nodes.tradeoff: Slightly less familiar syntax, but safer key handling.

nodes.title: Count each character from s
nodes.why: This builds the target frequency profile in one pass.
nodes.alternative: Sort both strings and compare directly.
nodes.tradeoff: O(n) counting is faster than O(n log n) sorting.

nodes.title: Stop immediately if a char is missing
nodes.why: If t asks for a char that s does not have enough of, it cannot be an anagram.
nodes.alternative: Finish loop and validate map at the end.
nodes.tradeoff: Early return keeps code efficient and easy to reason about.

nodes.title: Subtract counts while reading t
nodes.why: Each matching character cancels one counted character from s.
nodes.alternative: Create a second frequency map and compare maps.
nodes.tradeoff: Single map uses less memory and less code.

nodes.title: Return true if no mismatch happened
nodes.why: At this point lengths matched and every character from t was validated.
nodes.alternative: Run an extra loop to verify all counts are zero.
nodes.tradeoff: Given prior checks, extra verification is redundant here.
